NVDA.title: ‘The Big Short’ investor issues dire warning for Nvidia
NVDA.summary: Michael Burry, known for "The Big Short," is sounding the alarm on Nvidia, predicting an "aggressive fall" for the AI chipmaker. He cites concerning financial indicators, particularly Nvidia's high customer concentration, where its top three customers account for 64% of accounts receivable. Burry suggests lessons from this situation can apply to smaller businesses, advising caution in an increasingly frothy AI sector.

NVDA.title: Generate Investment Management Ltd Purchases Shares of 27,804 Intuit Inc. $INTU
NVDA.summary: Generate Investment Management Ltd recently acquired 27,804 shares of Intuit Inc. during the first quarter, valued at approximately $12 million. Intuit exceeded Q1 earnings expectations and issued optimistic FY 2026 guidance. Despite some analyst target cuts, the stock maintains a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $511.35 and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.20.

NVDA.title: BI Asset Management Fondsmaeglerselskab A S Grows Position in Textron Inc. $TXT
NVDA.summary: BI Asset Management Fondsmaeglerselskab A S significantly increased its stake in Textron Inc. (NYSE:TXT) by 249.8% in the first quarter, now holding 68,234 shares valued at $5.98 million. This comes after Textron reported strong Q1 results, exceeding analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, and raising its FY 2026 guidance. While analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $101.18, the stock opened lower despite the positive news.

NVDA.title: S&P Global declares quarterly dividend of $0.97 per share
NVDA.summary: S&P Global announced its Q3 2026 cash dividend of $0.97 per share, payable on September 10, 2026, marking an annualized rate of $3.88 per share and a 0.95% dividend yield. The company also recently launched the UN Global Compact Screening Dataset and partnered with Cohere for AI integration, and introduced the Credit Memo Builder. Stifel reiterated a Buy rating with a $489 price target.

NVDA.title: 11,173 Shares in NVIDIA Corporation $NVDA Purchased by Advisortrust Partners LLC
NVDA.summary: Advisortrust Partners LLC recently acquired 11,173 shares of NVIDIA Corporation (NASDAQ:NVDA), valued at approximately $2.08 million, establishing it as the firm's 26th-largest position. This move reflects sustained institutional interest in NVIDIA, with other major investors also increasing their holdings. The company has demonstrated strong financial performance, reporting robust earnings and revenue, while also approving a significant share buyback program and increasing its quarterly dividend.

NVDA.title: Royalties & licensing: investors can win with ARM Holdings, RE Royalties and Franco-Nevada!
NVDA.summary: This article examines the investment potential in companies utilizing royalty and licensing business models, highlighting their high margins and stable income streams due to minimal operating risk and long-term contracts. It features three companies: Franco-Nevada in mining, RE Royalties in cleantech, and ARM Holdings in the chip industry, discussing their business approaches, financial performance, and key advantages and disadvantages for investors. While Franco-Nevada offers stability and RE Royalties a high dividend yield, ARM Holdings presents significant growth potential but with a "priced to perfection" valuation.

NVDA.title: Is Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) Using Its Dividend To Signal Durable AI Power-Management Strength?
NVDA.summary: Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) recently affirmed a Q2 2026 cash dividend of US$2.00 per share, reinforcing its position as a consistent cash-returner amid rising sentiment for AI-related power management. This dividend, combined with a previous increase and strong Q2 revenue guidance, suggests the company's belief in sustained demand from AI data infrastructure. Investors should consider the company's revenue and earnings projections, potential valuation risks, and other analyst perspectives on its fair value.

NVDA.title: Arrow Electronics works with STMicroelectronics to accelerate industrial AMR development with a new reference platform
NVDA.summary: Arrow Electronics, in collaboration with its subsidiary eInfochips and STMicroelectronics, has launched a new industrial autonomous mobile robot (AMR) reference platform. This platform aims to streamline the development process for professional service robots by providing a pre-validated, integrated solution. It combines ST's industrial components with an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano compute platform, featuring robust power management, advanced motion control, and comprehensive sensing capabilities, designed to significantly reduce time to market for robotics OEMs and integrators.

MSFT.title: Apple and Tesla trade secrets reportedly exposed following a Tata Electronics cyberattack
MSFT.summary: A cyberattack on Tata Electronics, a manufacturing partner for Apple and Tesla, has reportedly exposed up to 630GB of trade secrets belonging to both American companies. The World Leaks ransomware group claims responsibility, posting over 200,000 files on the dark web, including component designs, specifications for Apple products, and documents related to Tesla's Project Highland and Model Y components. Although Tata Electronics stated its operations are unaffected, Apple has launched an investigation into the incident.

MSFT.title: IBM and OpenAI Partnership Shows Where AI May Start Generating Real Revenue
MSFT.summary: IBM and OpenAI have announced a partnership focusing on leveraging advanced AI models for enterprise cybersecurity. This collaboration addresses the shortage of qualified cybersecurity professionals and the increasing volume of threats, indicating a shift towards real-world monetization of AI in business applications. The move is significant for both companies, allowing OpenAI to expand into the enterprise market and IBM to enhance its security offerings, signaling the next phase of AI growth in application layers.

MSFT.title: Jim Cramer Reveals What He’d Like To See With Microsoft (MSFT)
MSFT.summary: Jim Cramer expressed concern about Microsoft's recent stock performance, particularly regarding its earnings momentum. He highlighted the stock's weakness amidst market turbulence, partially attributed to SpaceX's IPO. While analysts remain optimistic about Microsoft's AI advancements, Cramer is looking for stronger earnings growth from the company.

MSFT.title: Royalties & licensing: investors can win with ARM Holdings, RE Royalties and Franco-Nevada!
MSFT.summary: This article examines the investment potential in companies utilizing royalty and licensing business models, highlighting their high margins and stable income streams due to minimal operating risk and long-term contracts. It features three companies: Franco-Nevada in mining, RE Royalties in cleantech, and ARM Holdings in the chip industry, discussing their business approaches, financial performance, and key advantages and disadvantages for investors. While Franco-Nevada offers stability and RE Royalties a high dividend yield, ARM Holdings presents significant growth potential but with a "priced to perfection" valuation.

MSFT.title: Meltwater Pricing & Alternatives (2026)
MSFT.summary: This article compares Meltwater with ACCESS Newswire, highlighting their pricing, features, and capabilities in the PR and media intelligence space. Meltwater is presented as primarily a monitoring and intelligence platform with outsourced press release distribution and higher costs, while ACCESS Newswire is positioned as a comprehensive, all-in-one PR and IR solution with its own distribution network and transparent pricing. The comparison details specific differences in media databases, AI tools, social listening, and investor relations offerings.

MSFT.title: Coca-Cola Shakes Up $20 Billion Tax Dispute
MSFT.summary: Coca-Cola is involved in a decade-long $20 billion tax dispute with the IRS regarding how it handles cross-border transactions and licensing fees for its intellectual property. The IRS alleges Coca-Cola underreported its US taxable income by charging low licensing fees to overseas units. This week, a federal appeals court will hear arguments in the case, which could have significant implications for multinational companies, as other firms like Airbnb, Newell Brands, Microsoft, and Amgen face similar IRS scrutiny.

MSFT.title: Tech giant Oracle cuts 21,000 jobs as it embraces AI
MSFT.summary: Oracle has reduced its global workforce by 21,000 roles, approximately 13%, over the past year, as it realigns its business toward artificial intelligence (AI). The company reported that the deployment of AI technologies has led to these job reductions, resulting in $1.8 billion in severance and restructuring costs. This move is part of a broader trend among tech giants, including Amazon and Meta, which are investing heavily in AI infrastructure while simultaneously reducing their workforces.

MSFT.title: Credo Technology (CRDO) Earns New Analyst Upgrade, Shares Climb to Fresh Peak
MSFT.summary: Credo Technology (CRDO) shares surged to an all-time high after BNP Paribas issued a bullish coverage report, citing the company's strong position to benefit from the artificial intelligence buildout with its copper and optical interconnects. BNP Paribas believes Credo's total addressable market could expand to over $10 billion, driven by innovations like active electrical cables and silicon photonics. Despite the positive outlook, BNP set a price target of $275, which is lower than Monday's closing price, while also noting the company's diversifying customer base including five of six hyperscalers.

GOOGL.title: Evolve Private Wealth LLC Has $2.16 Million Holdings in Blackstone Inc. $BX
GOOGL.summary: Evolve Private Wealth LLC significantly increased its stake in Blackstone Inc. during the first quarter, now holding 18,802 shares valued at approximately $2.16 million. Blackstone reported strong quarterly earnings, surpassing EPS expectations with $1.36 and achieving 10% revenue growth year-over-year. The company offers a quarterly dividend of $1.16, providing a 3.8% yield, despite a high payout ratio.

GOOGL.title: S&P Global declares quarterly dividend of $0.97 per share
GOOGL.summary: S&P Global announced its Q3 2026 cash dividend of $0.97 per share, payable on September 10, 2026, marking an annualized rate of $3.88 per share and a 0.95% dividend yield. The company also recently launched the UN Global Compact Screening Dataset and partnered with Cohere for AI integration, and introduced the Credit Memo Builder. Stifel reiterated a Buy rating with a $489 price target.

GOOGL.title: Qualcomm Makes Another Move in AI Chess Game with $4 Billion Bet on Modular
GOOGL.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire AI chip startup Modular Inc. for approximately $4 billion, building on recent negotiations for another AI chip company, Tenstorrent. If both deals materialize, Qualcomm would spend nearly $14 billion to significantly expand its AI chip portfolio, positioning itself to compete more directly with Nvidia, AMD, and Intel. The company's intensified AI strategy and potential acquisitions are expected to be key topics at its upcoming investor day.

GOOGL.title: US stock futures fall as tech remains skittish after Alphabet, SpaceX losses
GOOGL.summary: US stock index futures declined on Monday evening, primarily driven by continued skittishness in the technology sector following significant losses in Alphabet and SpaceX. Investors are also monitoring ongoing U.S.-Iran peace negotiations, which have shown some progress, and upcoming economic data, including purchasing managers index data, revised GDP figures, and the PCE price index, for clues on interest rate policy.

GOOGL.title: Form 144 HEWLETT PACKARD ENTERPRISE COMPANY For: 22 June By Investing.com
GOOGL.summary: This article reports on a Form 144 filing by HEWLETT PACKARD ENTERPRISE COMPANY for June 22. It briefly mentions the company and its stock performance. The article also includes various market data and other news headlines.

GOOGL.title: BuzzFeed director Chris Malone buys $24,999 in class A stock
GOOGL.summary: BuzzFeed director Chris Malone recently purchased 17,361 shares of the company's Class A Common Stock for $24,999 on June 17, 2026. This transaction strengthens Malone's direct holdings to 17,361 shares. The purchase follows a significant 55% surge in BuzzFeed's stock over the past six months, despite the company operating with a considerable debt burden and experiencing rapid cash burn, as highlighted by InvestingPro analysis.

GOOGL.title: Synaptics Highlights Coral Board Edge AI Applications at Google I/O 2026
GOOGL.source: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media
GOOGL.summary: Synaptics showcased its Coral Board, an edge AI development platform, at Google I/O 2026. This platform, powered by Synaptics' Astra SL2610 processors and integrating Google's Coral NPU technology, enables on-device AI applications. At the event, Synaptics demonstrated an AI music experience called Jellectronica, using the Coral Board to generate music in real time based on jellyfish movements, and highlighted its capability to accelerate Google's Gemma 3 270M model for various edge AI tasks.

GOOGL.title: Form DEF 14A Alphabet Inc Class A For: 22 June By Investing.com
GOOGL.summary: This article announces the filing of Form DEF 14A by Alphabet Inc. Class A (GOOGL) for June 22. It is a brief, factual notification regarding a regulatory filing related to the company. The content primarily consists of the headline and a repetition of the announcement.
